locus_reconciliation.bracket_label: [#327 bf16 lm_head+attn floor, #326 off-the-shelf VBI upper]
locus_reconciliation.rationale: #326 (io4cs2ch): int4-Marlin body bit-exact across M (maxdiff 0.0) AND bf16 lm_head+attn carries 100% of the M=8 divergence -> #216's 0.9455% (scoped to the M-invariant int4 body) is NOT the identity-divergence locus. #324 (pespixw1) originated the divergence bisection; #327 (kcjlr5ny) prices the correct bf16 lm_head+attn locus at 9.841%.

named_points.note: #216 int4-Marlin split-K 0.95% -- the WRONG locus #354 used (int4 body is bit-exact across M, #326)
#327 convex-sub-optimistic 6.22% -- needs >25.5% SDPA recovery; #332 shows only 7.5% -> REFUTED
#332 geometric-phi 9.10% -- phi_realizable>=1 pushes the realizable floor toward 9.841%
#327 bf16 lm_head+attn full-slack floor 9.841% -- the CORRECT-locus eta (headline)

robustness.note: the 9.841% floor charges the FULL forgone SDPA split-KV slack; the convex hedge 6.22% would need a kernel recovering >25.5% of it, but #332 measures only 7.5% geometric recovery with phi_realizable>=1 (occupancy-saturated 3D split-KV) -> NO recovery -> the floor is robust. Across the entire [6.22%, 31.41%] bracket the corrected base-lift target exceeds the lambda=1 ceiling headroom, so the 'requires sub-int4 ceiling-lift' verdict never flips -> Tier-2 GPU measurement is NOT decision-relevant (would only refine the point estimate within [9.10%, 9.841%]).

synthesis.verdict: LOCUS-CORRECTED -> 500 BEYOND THE CURRENT CEILING. The identity-restoring kernel must determinize the bf16 lm_head+attn reduction, NOT the int4 body: #326 measured the int4-Marlin body BIT-EXACT across M (maxdiff 0.0) with 100% of the M=8 divergence in the bf16 lm_head+attn locus, so #354's int4-split-K eta=0.9455% is the WRONG locus. The CORRECT eta is #327's bf16 lm_head+attn first-principles floor 9.841% (10.41x larger). Re-pricing: strict-frontier@deployed = 434.14 TPS (vs #354's 476.98), corrected_base_lift_target = +73.05 TPS (vs #354's +23.24; 3.14x harder). DECISIVE: that +73.0 EXCEEDS the entire headroom to the lambda=1 ceiling (+39.42); even lifting the base to the FULL ceiling 520.95 yields only 469.68 TPS (== #327's banked 469.68) < 500. So a base-lift toward the existing ceiling CANNOT close 500; a sub-int4 CEILING-lift raising 520.95 itself by >= +33.62 TPS (to >= 554.58) is REQUIRED. The verdict is INVARIANT across the entire [6.22%, 31.41%] bracket (even the optimistic-but-refuted 6.22% needs +51.6 > +39.4), and #332 shows the geometric recovery (7.5%) is far below the 25.5% a revival needs (phi_realizable>=1) -> the floor is ROBUST. Tier-2 GPU NOT triggered: a measured eta would only refine [9.10%, 9.841%] without flipping the verdict. eta_locus_is_bf16_lmhead_attn=True. 0 GPU, 0 TPS, baseline 481.53 UNCHANGED. Bank-the-analysis.
synthesis.handoff: the strict identity kernel's eta is governed by the bf16 lm_head+attn reduction (9.841% floor, #327), NOT the int4 split-K (0.95%, #354) -- the int4 body is bit-exact across M (#326). The REAL base-lift target the sub-int4 lever (denken #356 / lawine #355) and the step-shave (kanna #359) must hit is +73.0 TPS (not #354's +23), which EXCEEDS the +39 headroom to the lambda=1 ceiling: even the full ceiling base (521) lands at 470 < 500. The strict program therefore needs a sub-int4 CEILING-lift of >= +34 TPS above 521 (a faster substrate, not a closure toward the existing ceiling). fern #357 (composite integrator) consumes this eta + target; the final strict-frontier figure still needs the approval-gated a10g path (481.53 is the a10g-scorer number).

scope: CPU-only analytic over banked MERGED *_results.json (#354 mr9dvb0e, #326 io4cs2ch, #327 kcjlr5ny, #332 y5cl0ena). NO model build / training / served-file change / HF Job / submission. 0 GPU, 0 TPS. BASELINE 481.53 UNCHANGED. Bank-the-analysis.
